title: Area of Parallelograms
contentObjective: I can find the area of a parallelogram using base × height.
languageObjective: I can explain how I found the area using the words base, height, area, and formula.

turnAndTalk.question: Look at the patio blueprint with a base of 14 feet and a height of 9 feet. Why do we use the height (9 ft) and not the slanted side to find the area?
turnAndTalk.kernel: We use the height because it is perpendicular to the base.

stems.en: We use the height because it is ___ to the base.
stems.es: Usamos la altura porque es ___ a la base.

stems.en: The slanted side is not the height because ___.
stems.es: El lado inclinado no es la altura porque ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students connect 'height' to the perpendicular (straight-up) distance from base to top, and recognize the slanted side is longer and would overstate the area.
turnAndTalk.extend: If the client tilted the patio to make it more slanted but kept the base at 14 ft and the height at 9 ft, would the area change? Justify your answer.
extendStems: The area would ___ because the formula only depends on ___ and ___.
Tilting the shape changes the slanted side but not the ___, so ___.

turnAndTalk.question: On the grid you cut a triangle off one end of the parallelogram and slid it to the other side. What shape did you make, and why does that prove the area is base x height?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Rearranging the parallelogram makes a rectangle with the same base and height.

stems.en: When I move the triangle, I get a ___.
stems.es: Cuando muevo el triángulo, obtengo un ___.

stems.en: Area equals base times ___, so 14 times 9 equals ___.
stems.es: El área es la base por ___, entonces 14 por 9 es ___.

stems.en: It is like a rectangle because ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: A strong answer names a rectangle, states the area is 14 x 9 = 126 sq ft, and explains the rearranged rectangle has the same base and height as the parallelogram.
turnAndTalk.extend: A classmate says any quadrilateral's area is base x height. Critique this claim using what you know about parallelograms versus other four-sided shapes.
extendStems: This claim is wrong because ___ only works when ___.
A counterexample is ___, which shows that ___.

turnAndTalk.question: The landscape architect tiles an 18 ft by 10 ft parallelogram courtyard and each tile covers 2 sq ft. Talk through how you find the number of tiles.
turnAndTalk.kernel: First find the area, then divide by the tile size.

stems.en: First I find the area: 18 times 10 equals ___ square feet.
stems.es: Primero hallo el área: 18 por 10 es ___ pies cuadrados.

stems.en: Then I divide by 2 because ___, so she needs ___ tiles.
stems.es: Luego divido entre 2 porque ___, entonces necesita ___ baldosas.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students compute area = 180 sq ft, then divide 180 / 2 = 90 tiles, and explain why dividing by the tile size gives the count.
turnAndTalk.extend: Tiles come in boxes of 12. Generalize a rule for finding how many full boxes the architect must buy for any courtyard area, and apply it here.
extendStems: A rule for boxes is ___ because ___.
For this courtyard she needs ___ boxes, since ___.

vocabulary.definition: A four-sided shape with two pairs of parallel sides.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una figura de cuatro lados con dos pares de lados paralelos.
vocabulary.visual: Think of a leaning door — the top and bottom are parallel, and the two sides are parallel, like a slanted rectangle
vocabulary.cloze: A quadrilateral with two pairs of parallel sides is a ___.

examples.text: A slanted four-sided shape with both pairs of sides parallel
examples.why: Both pairs of opposite sides are parallel.
A triangle has three sides, not two pairs of parallel sides.

vocabulary.definition: A side of the shape you use to find the area.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Un lado de la figura que usas para hallar el área.
vocabulary.visual: If the bottom of a parallelogram is 10 cm, then b = 10 cm in the formula A = b × h
vocabulary.cloze: Any side of a parallelogram used to calculate its area is the ___.

vocabulary.definition: The straight-up distance from the base to the top.
vocabulary.definitionEs: La distancia recta hacia arriba desde la base hasta la parte de arriba.
vocabulary.visual: A dashed vertical line from the top side straight down to the base, forming a 90° angle — NOT the slanted side
vocabulary.cloze: The perpendicular distance between the base and the opposite side is the ___.

vocabulary.definition: How much space is inside a flat shape.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Cuánto espacio hay dentro de una figura plana.
vocabulary.visual: A 3 cm × 4 cm rectangle covers 12 square centimeters — imagine 12 tiny 1×1 squares inside it
vocabulary.cloze: The amount of flat space inside a two-dimensional figure is its ___.

vocabulary.definition: A shape made by putting two or more simple shapes together.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una figura formada al juntar dos o más figuras simples.
vocabulary.visual: An L-shaped room = a 10×8 rectangle joined to a 4×5 rectangle; total area = 80 + 20 = 100 sq ft
vocabulary.cloze: A shape made of two or more basic shapes combined is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: A math rule written with symbols.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una regla matemática escrita con símbolos.
vocabulary.visual: A = b × h means Area equals base times height; for a parallelogram with b = 6 and h = 4, A = 24
vocabulary.cloze: A math rule written with symbols, like A = b × h, is a ___.

launch.narrative: Your architecture firm is designing a parallelogram-shaped patio for a client's backyard. The client needs to know how many square feet of pavers to order. The patio has a base of 14 feet and a height of 9 feet.
launch.contextImage: Architectural blueprint with a parallelogram-shaped patio outlined in green
noticePrompts: How is a parallelogram different from a rectangle?
wonderPrompts: Why do we use the height instead of the slanted side to find the area?
Can you rearrange a parallelogram into a rectangle?

conceptIntro.heading: How do we find the area of a parallelogram?
conceptIntro.intro: A parallelogram is a four-sided shape with two pairs of parallel sides, like a leaning rectangle. Its area is how much flat space is inside it.
conceptIntro.keyIdea: Area of a parallelogram = base × height, where the height is the straight-up (perpendicular) distance, NOT the slanted side.

iDo.title: Watch me
lines: I have a parallelogram with a base of 14 feet and a height of 9 feet.
I write the formula: A = base × height.
I put in the numbers: A = 14 × 9.
So the area is 126 square feet. I used the height of 9, not the slanted side.

weDo.title: Let's try together
lines: Now a smaller parallelogram: base = 5 cm, height = 4 cm.
What is the formula? A = base × height.
What do we put in? A = 5 × 4.
What is 5 × 4? Yes, 20, so the area is 20 square centimeters.

youDo.title: Now it's your turn
lines: Next you will plot the patio on a grid and find its area yourself.
Remember: multiply the base by the height, and always use the perpendicular height.

explore.instructions: Plot the vertices of a parallelogram with base 14 and height 9. Place points at (0, 0), (14, 0), (16, 9), and (2, 9) to visualize the patio.
explore.label: Plot the four vertices of the parallelogram-shaped patio.

discourse.prompt: If you cut a triangle from one side of the parallelogram and move it to the other side, what shape do you get? How does this help you find the area?
discourse.sentenceFrame: The area of the parallelogram equals base x height = ___ x ___ = ___ square feet, which is the same as a ___ with the same base and height.

optional.stem: In the design lab you cut a triangle off the slanted left end of a parallelogram blueprint and slide it to the right end. What shape do you make, and why does it prove A = base x height?
choices: A rectangle with the same base and height, so its area equals base x height
A bigger parallelogram, so the area gets larger
A triangle, so the area is cut in half
A square, so all four sides must be equal
optional.explanation: Sliding the cut triangle turns the parallelogram into a rectangle that keeps the same base and height. A rectangle's area is base x height, so the parallelogram's area is base x height too.

optional.stem: A blueprint parallelogram has a base of 8 ft, a height of 5 ft, and a slanted side of 7 ft. What is its area?
optional.explanation: Area = base x height = 8 x 5 = 40 sq ft. The 7 ft slanted side is not used; only the perpendicular height belongs in the formula.

optional.instructions: Each label describes a segment on a parallelogram blueprint. Sort each one into 'Is the height' or 'Is NOT the height'.

items.text: A dashed line straight up from the base, making a 90 degree angle
The slanted left edge of the parallelogram
The perpendicular distance from the base to the top side
The long tilted side that connects the bottom to the top
The straight-up distance measured at a right angle to the base
The leaning side that is longer than the perpendicular gap

optional.label: Match each parallelogram's base and height to its correct area (A = base x height).

optional.stem: Two blueprints share a base of 10 ft. Blueprint M has height 6 ft; Blueprint N is more tilted with the same height of 6 ft but a longer slanted side of 9 ft. How do their areas compare?
choices: Equal: both are 10 x 6 = 60 sq ft because area depends on base and height, not the slant
Blueprint N is larger because its slanted side is 9 ft
Blueprint M is larger because it is less tilted
You cannot compare them without the slanted side of M
optional.explanation: Area = base x height. Both have base 10 ft and height 6 ft, so both areas are 10 x 6 = 60 sq ft. Tilting the shape changes the slanted side but not the perpendicular height, so the area stays the same.

optional.title: Level 2 Extension — The Slanted-Side Slip-Up

workedExample.label: Read the blueprint
workedExample.work: base = 12 ft, slanted side = 10 ft, height = 7 ft

optional.correctWork: The height is 7 ft, not the slanted side of 10 ft. Substitute the perpendicular height: A = 12 x 7 = 84 sq ft. Only the straight-up height belongs in A = base x height.
hints: Look at the substitution step. Which number did the designer plug in for the height -- the slanted side or the real height?
The height must be perpendicular (a 90 degree angle) to the base. The 10 ft slanted side is not the height, so use 7 ft instead.

approaching.stem: What is the area of a parallelogram with base 10 cm and height 7 cm?
A parallelogram has an area of 54 sq ft and a base of 9 ft. What is the height?
What is the area of a parallelogram with base 9 cm and height 5 cm?
Which measurement is the height of a parallelogram?
choices: The perpendicular distance between the base and the opposite side
The length of the slanted side
approaching.explanation: The height is always the perpendicular (straight up-and-down) distance between the base and the opposite side, not the slanted side.

approaching.instructions: Use the formula A = b × h to find the area of each parallelogram.

onLevel.label: Match each shape to its area.

onLevel.instructions: Sort each parallelogram by whether its area is greater than 50 sq units or 50 or less.
A parallelogram has base 8 ft. Place a point on the number line at the height that gives an area of 48 sq ft. (Hint: A = b × h, so h = A ÷ b.)
onLevel.label: Find the missing height: b = 8, A = 48

extending.title: Find the Area Error

workedExample.label: Identify base and height
workedExample.work: b = 11 m, slant side = 8 m, h = 6 m

extending.correctWork: The height is 6 m, not the slant side of 8 m. A = 11 × 6 = 66 sq m. Always use the perpendicular height, not the slanted side.
hints: Look at Step 3. Which value should be used for h — the slant side or the height?
The height must be perpendicular to the base. The slant side is not the height.

extending.prompt: A parallelogram has a base of 12 cm. If you double the height, the area doubles too. Explain why this happens using the formula, and give a specific example with numbers.
extending.sentenceFrame: When the height doubles from ___ to ___, the area changes from ___ to ___ because in the formula A = b × h, the base stays at ___ and ___.

extending.instructions: Plot a parallelogram with base 10 and height 6 on the grid. Then calculate its area and compare it to the rectangle 10 × 6.
extending.label: Plot the parallelogram and find its area.

optionalActivity.name: Parallelogram Builder Lab
optionalActivity.intro: Step into the firm's design lab and prove every parallelogram blueprint hides a rectangle inside it.

connect.scenario: A landscape architect is tiling a parallelogram-shaped courtyard. The courtyard has a base of 18 feet and a height of 10 feet. Each tile covers 2 square feet.
connect.promptQuestion: How many tiles does the architect need?
connect.prompt: The courtyard's area is ___ sq ft because A = ___ x ___ . She needs ___ tiles because ___ / 2 = ___.

exitTicket.stem: A parallelogram has a base of 13 inches and a height of 5 inches. What is its area?
exitTicket.explanation: A = b x h = 13 x 5 = 65 square inches.

projects.title: Unit 5 Project — Area in Action
projects.desc: Capstone: apply area of polygons and composite figures to a real design challenge.

projects.title: Area Architect — City Builder
projects.desc: Build a city by calculating areas of parallelograms, triangles, trapezoids, and composite figures.

projects.title: Graphic Novel #1 — Theme Park Engineer
projects.desc: Story-driven area mission across parallelograms, triangles, and trapezoids.
